title: Justicia ellisii
intro: Justicia ellisii is a flowering plant species belonging to the Acanthaceae family, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Justicia ellisii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and capsules, which are consumed to alleviate respiratory issues and boost immune function.
taxonomy: Justicia ellisii is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It belongs to the genus Justicia and is a part of the subfamily Acanthoideae. The plant's classification at the species level is Justicia ellisii. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships within the Acanthaceae family. Further classification at the infraspecific level may be necessary to fully understand the plant's taxonomy.
